title: Going after SEELE
wikitext:
[]: Going after SEELE             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 18, 2010 7:52 pm Okay. So Third Impact has happened, and most of the people who really want to have managed to find their way back to the world. Let's say peace and order are somehow restored (quiet, Tines! ). Let's also say some iteration of NERV/the UN gets up and running. First order of business? Well, fixing the damn world. Second order of business? See above. Nth order of business: holding those responsible for Third Impact accountable. That's mainly SEELE. Problem: the bastards are hiding out in the LCL. So how do you force them out? I know it's not really addressed in the series, but how would you do it? Or, if you think it's impossible, why?

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Thu Nov 18, 2010 8:03 pm 1) How about asking them to come out or we burn all the LCL away, in an attempt to destroy them all? (sure, many innocent souls would be destroyed as well, but hey, if we can make threats to the bastards we can also make "suggestions" to others, right? Like "hey guys, you just might want to come back from tang Real Soon Now") 2) Use something like the thing Ritsuko did when trying to pry Shinji out of Eva-01 after Zeruel?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 18, 2010 8:14 pm BiQ wrote: 2) Use something like the thing Ritsuko did when trying to pry Shinji out of Eva-01 after Zeruel?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Nov 18, 2010 8:21 pm Bagheera wrote: That's what I had in mind, but what does that entail? I'm still really fuzzy on that.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 18, 2010 8:27 pm Azathoth wrote: Bit of a moot point, isn't it? First of all, the salvaging device was in the Geofront. Second of all, it doesn't work.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Nov 18, 2010 8:44 pm What above Nerv? Are they not to be held at all accountable for what happened, at least the higher ranking officers.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 18, 2010 8:53 pm Sachi wrote: What above Nerv? Are they not to be held at all accountable for what happened, at least the higher ranking officers.

[]:             Quote Post by lilim » Fri Nov 19, 2010 1:33 am would SEELE and the higher ups at NERV even want to come back? if SEELE wanted to evolve, and Gendo and Fuyutsuki just wanted to be with Yui, it doesn't seem like they would have much motivation to return, would they? if i'm wrong about their motives, PLEASE enlighten me, as i've only seen the series and EoE once and am still fuzzy about what the Human Instrumentality Project really means to both of them. i got that Gendo wanted to see Yui in EoE when he tries to merge Adam with Rei, but how did he think that was going to happen? and i'd assumed Fuyutsuki's compliance had a lot to do with Yui. (though of course from the scene of him telling her he wasn't going to pull any more stunts, and they could have him whacked off pretty easily, i got the feeling he was also going along with it so that he wouldn't end up in a ditch somewhere..) as far as actually getting them out, i like the "How about asking them to come out or we burn all the LCL away, in an attempt to destroy them all?" idea that BiQ had. and i'd love to see Misato and Kaji work together. i really loved the way those two interacted, i think they're both badass and even if i wasn't a Kaji/Misato fan, as a team they're still awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Fri Nov 19, 2010 1:49 am I don't think the old SEELE farts would ever try to return. They wanted to be assimilated into Instrumentality, having forsaken any ideas of living in a corporeal existence, insisting that Instrumentality is a dimension where pain and suffering is unknown and thus they simply embraced it.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Nov 19, 2010 2:06 am Well, it's up for some debate whether Instrumentality as a singular consciousness at peace (as opposed to just a bunch of disembodied souls floating around aimlessly in confusion) even existed anymore after Shinji rejected it. I forget what the usual consensus is, if there is any.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Nov 19, 2010 2:37 am Kaworu said that AT Fields would once again separate people if Shinji chose to, and he did. That seems to imply that there's no more of the one-beingness that was instrumentality, which kind of leads to the question of what are the rest of the souls doing floating around by themselves somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:39 am soul.assassin wrote: I don't think the old SEELE farts would ever try to return. They wanted to be assimilated into Instrumentality, having forsaken any ideas of living in a corporeal existence, insisting that Instrumentality is a dimension where pain and suffering is unknown and thus they simply embraced it.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Fri Nov 19, 2010 7:33 am Bagheera wrote: Yes, I know. I started the topic looking for ways to force them to return.

[]:             Quote Post by lilim » Fri Nov 19, 2010 10:28 am it's exciting to think of everyone going after the old men of SEELE and giving them what they deserve but considering the physical and emotional state everyone would be in, i think anything after 3rd Impact/Instrumentality would be fucked up anyway without even going into that. assuming Misato and Kaji come back, they're probably going to be spent for a long time. wouldn't it take an enormous amount of energy just to try to rebuild yourself and figure what happened to you and how you're going to survive? an amount of energy that maybe wouldn't be firstly and foremost put into "let's find who did this and break their necks" but more like "i have no idea what just happened to me and i feel like shit in every possible way and i've gone nearly crazy and i need to eat"? but what soul.assassin said is a good idea, it's the only thing i can think of that would make SEELE want to come back. maybe realizing that they lost and being infuriated, they're adamant (and stupid) enough to try to do it again, their way, so they come back. at the same time, if they did at least experience Human Instrumentality, however brief, maybe that was enough for them. maybe they didn't stay mentally stable for long enough to figure out that it ended. not that they were really.. "stable" to begin with. but i think you know what i mean. i guess it would depend on how much the souls knew of what was happening when Shinji rejects Instrumentality. what exactly happens to them, or moreover, what do they think is happening to them?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Nov 19, 2010 11:21 am I think by the time any sort of civilization is reconstructed and order is restored all concern for payback will have slipped to the back of the minds of the people. For all intents and purposes, Seele is dead and nobody cares.

[]:             Quote Post by lilim » Fri Nov 19, 2010 11:37 am i could kind of see after everyone got back to a more normal state and were getting by, Misato and Kaji realizing that SEELE was back, they'd probably check it out but i'd think SEELE would be in dire straits as it is. they'd have, at the least, lost a lot of manpower and they'd probably be a sad little pile of scared children trying to survive by that point. you wouldn't even really need to try to get them in a position of frailty because at that point everyone would be vulnerable. i don't think that the thought of revenge wouldn't occur to those who knew of SEELE but it would probably be a matter of "they're already being punished, if we don't help them, they'll die" and they'd probably simply choose not to help them. (at least Misato and Kaji) because i would think that the only way you could attempt to restore humanity in any way would be to get everyone who was left working together. i don't think individuals have much of a chance, and especially if only certain members came back. there's safety in numbers, but if you're a malicious old cult who's returned to a destroyed shell of a world, the only people living wanting nothing to do with you--wouldn't that be what they deserved? certainly better than just killing them. it may be satisfying to, but this is far more cruel in the long run.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Nov 19, 2010 12:18 pm Sachi wrote: For all intents and purposes, Seele is dead and nobody cares.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Nov 19, 2010 3:45 pm Pretty much. Besides, who cares? Even if they somehow knew what was responsible, they have way bigger priorities, and it's not like there was THAT much damage done. It seems like some people might've gotten some therapeutic use out of it all.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 19, 2010 4:46 pm soul.assassin wrote: Let's see... What if, somewhere, one or a few of them realize it's too late, or that Instrumentality turns out to be fake, a nightmare? The first thing the late dissenters will have in their minds is to will themselves to go back to existence. And if they manage to come out of the ocean... well, if the people of the world know of what they have wrought... expect a lynch mob.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Fri Nov 19, 2010 4:52 pm Bagheera wrote: Also, I can imagine many folks might consider hunting them down and executing them to be a damn fine form of therapy.
